title: shattering briny dutch beef pancakes
description: A sick-day dutch dish featuring ground beef, caramelized onions, and a crunchy egg topping. Low-glycemic-friendly and perfect for a lazy day
instructions: In a large pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the diced onions and cook until caramelized, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes.
Add the ground beef to the pan, breaking it up with a spoon, and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the shredded carrots and cabbage to the pan and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the beef broth, salt, and black pepper, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and shredded cheddar cheese.
Add the almond flour to the bowl and stir until a crumbly mixture forms.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the beef mixture into a hot non-stick skillet or Dutch oven, forming a thick pancake.
Top each pancake with a spoonful of the egg mixture and cook until the eggs are set and the pancake is golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Repeat with the remaining beef and egg mixtures, making 8-10 pancakes in total.
Serve the pancakes hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or chives, if desired.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
